title: Cisco Voice Portal Dialed Number Pattern
description: Schema for a CVP Dialed Number Pattern (DNP). Dialed number patterns define how incoming calls are routed to specific VXML applications or call treatments based on the dialed number (DNIS). DNPs are a core configuration element in CVP that maps incoming numbers to call handling logic and integrates with ICM/UCCE routing.

pattern.description: The dialed number pattern. Supports wildcards (* for any digits). Examples: 18005551234, 1800555*, *

description.description: Description of what this pattern routes to

callType.description: Call type determining how the call interacts with ICM/UCCE. Pre-route calls are sent to ICM before any treatment. Translation-route calls receive self-service treatment then route to ICM. Post-route calls are fully handled by CVP without ICM.

applicationName.description: VXML application associated with this pattern

priority.description: Pattern matching priority (lower number = higher priority)

sipServerGroup.description: SIP server group for outbound routing

ringtoneApplication.description: Application to play while waiting for ICM route response

errorApplication.description: Application to play on error conditions

survivalApplication.description: Application to use in ICM survival mode (when ICM is unreachable)
